name: did_key_from_ed25519_pub
description: did:key URI derivation from a raw Ed25519 public key. Algorithm: prepend multicodec varint 0xed 0x01 (ed25519-pub) to the 32-byte pubkey, base58btc-encode the 34-byte payload, prefix with 'z' (multibase base58btc indicator), prefix with 'did:key:'. Same pubkey as ed25519_sign_verify vector — implementations can cross-reference.

assertions.multicodec_for_ed25519_pub: 0xed 0x01 (two-byte varint — 0xed is the unsigned-varint-encoded 237 which maps to ed25519-pub in the multicodec registry)

notes: The leading 'z' after 'did:key:' is a multibase indicator — z = base58btc. NOT part of the base58 output itself.

The 0xed multicodec ID is a varint. For values ≤ 127 it's a single byte; 0xed (237) needs two bytes: 0xed 0x01. Full varint decode: (0xed & 0x7f) | ((0x01) << 7) = 0x6d | 0x80 = 0xed = 237 — matches the ed25519-pub codec ID.

base58btc uses the Bitcoin alphabet — 58 chars excluding 0 (zero), O (capital-O), I (capital-i), l (lowercase-L). Decoding MUST preserve leading zero bytes as leading '1' characters; our multicodec prefix 0xed avoids that edge case.
did:key is the same across every ed25519 keypair — this encoding is deterministic. An implementation that derives a different string for the same pubkey is non-conforming.

Second case uses an all-zero pubkey to pin the output for edge-case pubkeys. All-zero is not a valid Ed25519 key (it's the identity point) but the did:key encoding doesn't validate curve points — it only encodes bytes.
